title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Colman Domingo hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Entertainment Colman Domingo hosted the April 11 episode of "Saturday Night Live," with Anitta as musical guest.
SNL recap: Host Colman Domingo appears before the April 11, 2026 episode of "Saturday Night Live.

" Rosalind O'Connor/NBC By Kevin Slane April 12, 2026 | 8:37 AM 2 minutes to read “SNL” returned to the air for the 167h episode of its 51st season on Saturday, April 11, with Colman Domingo as host and Anitta as musical guest.

title: Celtics’ signing of Dalano Banton officially completes Brad Stevens’s GM masterclass
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Celtics Stevens toed the luxury tax line to a tee down the stretch and still gave Joe Mazzulla a competitive roster this season.

title: Retired Mass. State Police sergeant pleads guilty in PPP fraud scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Local News Prosecutors say Damian Halfkenny, 54, of Boston, submitted false payroll information to get a COVID-19-era Paycheck Protection Program loan.
A retired Massachusetts State Police sergeant has agreed to plead guilty in connection with an alleged scheme to fraudulently obtain a federal pandemic relief loan, prosecutors announced Friday.
Damian Halfkenny, 54, of Boston, was charged with one count of wire fraud, U.

title: Former Boston police commissioner, off-duty cop, firefighter, and restaurant partner save choking man
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Local News Quick action at the Chestnut Hill Capital Grille is credited with saving an 80-year-old diner on Easter eve.
The Capital Grille's Large windows in the bar area open out onto Boylston Street.

Barry Chin/Globe Staff Photo Local off-duty first responders, the former Boston police commissioner, and a restaurant partner joined forces recently when an elderly man began severely choking while dining at a Chestnut Hill steakhouse.

title: Mass. high court allows AG’s lawsuit against Meta to move forward
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Local News As the state's new social media law comes closer to passing, Attorney General Andrea Campbell's lawsuit against Meta has been allowed to move forward.
